headline: Samsung Electronics (005930.KS) PLANS KRW 90 TRILLION (~$58.6B) SHARE BUYBACK per Yonhap — stock +6.3% Seoul, reclaims #1 Korea mcap from SK Hynix one session after KOSPI -9.99% circuit-breaker rout; buyback paired with chip-division employee stock bonuses (~10.5% of operating profit)
summary: Samsung Electronics plans a roughly KRW 90 trillion ($58.61B) share buyback programme, per Yonhap News Agency Wednesday morning Seoul time, after agreeing to a stock bonus for employees following recent wage talks. The program is partially driven by a May management/union pay deal under which Samsung is expected to set aside ~10.5% of operating profit for chip-division special bonuses in the form of stock — sparking concerns over inequality and forcing the offsetting buyback to neutralize dilution. Employee shares vest 1/3 immediately, 1/3 at year one, 1/3 at year two. Stock reaction: Samsung +6.3% Seoul session, outperforming SK Hynix +1.6% (later faded). The +6.3% move reclaimed the #1 Korea common-share market-cap crown back from SK Hynix only ONE TRADING DAY after Tuesday's KOSPI -9.99% circuit-breaker rout. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MASSIVE technical bid under 005930.KS — the largest treasury-stock retirement in Korean history dwarfs the 2017 KRW 9.3T program (~10x scale); (2) confirms management view that Samsung shares are undervalued post-Foundry order-book widening (AMD 2nm 2028, Google Axion/TPU, BYD AV SoC) and HBM3E/HBM4 ramp; (3) sympathetically positive for SK Hynix (000660.KS) — establishes a Korea-memory floor in the face of cycle-cresting worries; (4) the Korea-correlation drag on MU into tonight's Q3 print is now neutralized — Korea no longer the dominant fade-source.

headline: SK Hynix (000660.KS) CONFIRMS $29.4B U.S. ADR LISTING on Nasdaq — 17.79M new shares to issue at KRW 45.45 trillion; trading to begin TENTATIVELY July 10, 2026; largest non-domestic primary capital raise in Korean equity history
summary: SK Hynix Wednesday formally confirmed plans to raise approximately KRW 45.45 trillion (~$29.4B) via the listing of American Depositary Receipts on the Nasdaq, issuing 17.79 million new shares. The company expects trading to begin tentatively on July 10, 2026, though dates remain subject to change. SK Hynix said it anticipates the ADR listing will expand its investor base, 'ultimately allowing its true corporate value to be properly evaluated,' and elevate its status as a global company by broadening touchpoints in the U.S. — 'the epicenter of AI technological innovation.' Stock reaction: SK Hynix +1.6% Seoul session (later faded as initial buyback-relief rally peaked). Read-through to House coverage: (1) DOUBLE-EDGED — the $29.4B primary issue is technically dilutive but signals confidence in HBM3E/HBM4 demand durability requiring further capex; (2) STRONGLY POSITIVE structural setup for the Korean AI-memory cohort — institutional access via Nasdaq listing transforms 000660.KS from an EM trade requiring KRW account into a benchmarked U.S. exposure, supporting permanent valuation re-rate; (3) confirms read-through that Samsung's KRW 90T buyback is a defensive response to SK Hynix's structural capital edge; (4) positive sympathy for KOSPI semiconductor cohort — the Korean memory complex now has both passive Korea-flow and U.S. ADR-flow tailwinds; (5) capex pull-through positive for ASML, AMAT, LRCX, KLAC, Tokyo Electron (8035.T) ahead of Q2 prints.

headline: KOSPI REBOUNDS +3.26% to 8,471.02 Wednesday close — Samsung +6.3% on KRW 90T buyback news; SK Hynix +1.6% on $29B ADR plan; foreign short-cover unwind partially neutralizes Tuesday's -9.99% circuit breaker, though intraday gains FADED from +4.14% peak as memory-cycle anxiety persists into MU print
summary: The KOSPI closed Wednesday up +3.26% at 8,471.02 (+267.18 points) after rebounding from Tuesday's -9.99% circuit-breaker rout. Intraday peak was +4.14% before fading. Samsung Electronics surged +6.3% on the Yonhap KRW 90T buyback report; SK Hynix Inc rose +1.6% on the formal $29.4B ADR-listing confirmation (early +7% spike compressed by profit-taking). Kioxia (285A.T) Tokyo recovered from -15% Tuesday selloff. Key context: Tuesday's rout had been the worst single-session in KOSPI history with two circuit breakers in one day; today's rebound recovered roughly one-third of yesterday's losses. Foreign-investor flow turned net buyer after Tuesday's KRW 5.79T (~$3.8B) historic outflow. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEUTRAL-POSITIVE setup for MU into tonight's FQ3 print — Korea-correlation drag now neutralized but breadth-fragility evidence (intraday fade) means asymmetric risk persists if gross margin misses 80%; (2) the breadth crack from June 19 has now extended TWO days (June 23 down -9.99% / June 24 +3.26% fade) — the AI-memory supercycle's first credible technical correction now in the rearview but not fully repaired; (3) sympathetically positive for U.S. AI-infra cohort pre-open — Nasdaq futures +0.71% post-Korea close.

headline: FedEx (FDX) Q4 FY26 BEATS — $6.31 adj EPS vs $5.92 cons, $25.0B rev vs $24.01B cons (FIRST stand-alone parcel print post-Freight spinoff); BUT CY2026 GUIDE OF $16.90-$18.10 ADJ EPS DISAPPOINTS — STOCK -6.16% AH on stranded costs + pilot contract headwinds; closed regular session $316.83 (-3.63%), AH to $297.70
summary: FedEx reported fiscal Q4 2026 results Tuesday after close — its first quarterly print as a stand-alone parcel/express company following the June 1 spin-off of FedEx Freight (which paid a $4.1B cash dividend to FDX corp). Beats: adj EPS $6.31 vs $5.92 cons (+6.6%); revenue $25.0B vs $24.01B cons (+4.1%). Despite the beat, the stock fell ~3.6% in the regular session and ANOTHER 6.16% AH to $297.70 as the calendar-year 2026 guide of $16.90-$18.10 adj EPS (midpoint $17.50, implying ~20% growth in the transition period) and ~11% CY26 revenue growth came in below buyside whisper-bar. Headwinds cited: stranded costs from Freight separation, new pilot contract pay step-up, Network 2.0 execution noise during the transition period. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NEGATIVE INDUSTRIAL-LOGISTICS READ — soft FedEx guide cuts against the bull case for AI-buildout-driven industrial freight demand; yellow flag for VRT/ETN/GLW commercial-equipment shipments; (2) the FY27 guide includes 'jet fuel costs' and aviation network commentary — supports the energy-disinflation thesis (Brent ~$80 post-Iran roadmap); (3) parcel/express segment commentary did NOT call out outsized AI-datacenter shipment volume — modest dovish read for the Eaton/GE Vernova electrical-equipment supply chain into Q2 prints; (4) the disclosed pilot-contract step-up is a UPS / Atlas Air / Cathay sympathy yellow flag.

headline: Micron (MU) ENTERS Q3 FY26 PRINT (after close) +4.1% PRE-MARKET — Korea-correlation drag neutralized by Samsung buyback / SK Hynix ADR confirmation; 81% GM cliff-edge and HBM4 Vera Rubin 2027 allocation are THE acid tests; Strict consensus $19.95 EPS / $34.66B rev (LSEG $35.59B)
summary: Micron Technology rebounded +4.1% in pre-market Wednesday into tonight's after-close Q3 FY26 print, recovering from Tuesday's -13% Korea-correlation flush. Consensus: $19.95 EPS / $34.66B revenue (LSEG: $35.59B, above the top end of management's $32.75-34.25B guide range). The gross margin number is THE measure of the print: consensus 81.6% vs management's 81% guide — would be the highest in MU's 47-year history if achieved; cycle-cresting bears need sub-80% to win. HBM commentary on Vera Rubin Ultra 2027 allocations is the most critical forward-looking signal. Sell-side setup heading in: 27 Strong Buy / ZERO Sell, with PT scrambles topping at Stifel/UBS/Deutsche Bank $1,500 and Aletheia $1,600 — Goldman the lone bear at ~$400. Read-through to House coverage: (1) THE EVENT OF THE DAY for the AI-memory cohort — bull case requires both 81%+ gross margin AND constructive HBM4 capacity commentary; (2) any HBM4 ASP commentary frames the 005930.KS / 000660.KS rebound trade; (3) FY27 capex commentary is the on/off switch for AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/ASML/8035.T memory-WFE TAM; (4) Korea-correlation neutralization from Samsung's KRW 90T buyback removes the Tuesday-style flush risk but does not insulate from print-specific disappointment; (5) downstream read-through to MRVL, AVGO, ANET on HBM-bandwidth-fed networking demand.

headline: Oracle (ORCL) +1.5% PRE-MARKET WEDNESDAY as analysts re-frame 21,000-headcount-cut disclosure as MARGIN-DISCIPLINE positive — Jefferies five-star analyst reiterates 'Industry is sold out for any capacity within the next 6 months'; consensus 12-month PT $252.64 (~49% upside), 43 analyst Buy avg
summary: Oracle stock rebounded ~1.5% in Wednesday pre-market trading as the sell-side re-framed Monday's after-hours 21,000-layoff / $1.84B severance disclosure as a margin-discipline positive rather than the demand-pressure negative the initial -2% Tuesday tape implied. Key analyst voice: a five-star Jefferies analyst reiterated that 'industry is sold out for any capacity within the next 6 months,' supporting the view that OCI capacity is bid by hyperscaler demand even as ORCL trims sales/services headcount. Consensus across 43 analysts: average 'Buy' with 12-month price target $252.64 (implying ~49% upside from Tuesday's $170.32 low). Read-through to House coverage: (1) CONFIRMS the broader thesis that legacy enterprise software headcounts are being compressed by AI — supports IBM (JPM Overweight Tues), MSFT, NOW, CRM analogs; (2) positive read-through for CRWV / NBIS as the bid for OCI-style GPU capacity validates third-party AI-cloud TAM; (3) modestly positive for NVDA / AMD as Oracle's capex shift from headcount to silicon implies durable GPU procurement; (4) the Jefferies 'sold out' commentary is a sharp positive datapoint for the data-center power supply chain (VRT, ETN, GEV, GLW) — capacity sold means buildouts already booked must be completed.

headline: Powell DELIVERS HOUSE FINANCIAL SERVICES TESTIMONY TODAY — restates 'Fed well-positioned to learn more about price increases before changing interest rates'; flags June-July inflation prints as gate to first cut; CME July-cut probability slips to 18.8% from 22.7%
summary: Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell testifies before the House Financial Services Committee Wednesday morning, his first of two semi-annual Monetary Policy Report appearances. Powell reiterated that the Fed 'is well-positioned to learn more about price increases before changing interest rates' but signaled — in a marginally less-hawkish tone than recent commentary — that rate cuts could come once June and July CPI prints show 'what price increases and tariff pains are doing to American families.' Reaction: CME FedWatch July-cut probability slipped to 18.8% from 22.7% Tuesday — the testimony's net effect was modest hawkish surprise vs whisper-bar. Powell also restated his commitment to serving out his full term, brushing back Trump-administration pressure to resign early. Read-through to House coverage: (1) MODESTLY NEGATIVE for long-duration AI-infra / utility / SMR plays (CEG, VST, GEV, OKLO, SMR) which have benefited from rate-cut expectations; (2) marginally negative for high-multiple AI-software (PLTR, NOW, CRM) on duration sensitivity; (3) MODESTLY POSITIVE for cyclical capital-goods (ETN, ROK, VRT) where the implied steady-rate floor preserves nominal capex; (4) the tariff-pass-through commentary IS a yellow flag for semiconductor names paying 25% Section 232 duties on advanced-AI-chip exports — read-through to NVDA / AMD margin commentary on next earnings.

headline: Fed BANK STRESS TEST RESULTS RELEASE TODAY 4 PM ET for 32 large lenders — recession scenario assumes 10% UE, -30% home prices, -39% CRE — Fed pre-announced THIS YEAR'S RESULTS WILL NOT IMPACT CAPITAL REQUIREMENTS until 2027 model overhaul completes
summary: The Federal Reserve releases its 2026 Comprehensive Capital Analysis and Review (CCAR) stress-test results Wednesday at 4 PM ET, covering 32 of the largest U.S. banking organizations. The severely adverse scenario assumes unemployment rising to 10%, home prices declining 30%, commercial real estate falling 39%, plus a severe global recession. Critically, the Fed pre-announced in June that these results will NOT impact bank capital requirements — capital floors remain unchanged until 2027 while the Fed reviews and updates its stress-testing models. This effectively neutralizes the typical post-CCAR capital-return guidance event that historically drove bank-stock multiple expansion. Read-through to House coverage: (1) NOT a direct AI-Datacenter-Power name catalyst, but a meaningful CY26 capital-flow input — banks NOT seeing capital relief means less corporate-bond / TLAC issuance bid, marginally tighter financial conditions for AI-infra capex; (2) the lack of capital-return uplift is a mild yellow flag for the JPM Tuesday IBM-upgrade thesis (software AI-infra winners) since the financial-services digitization spend cycle is the cleanest read on enterprise AI uptake; (3) modestly positive for energy/utility cohort (CEG, VST) as bank-stock multiple-compression typically rotates flows toward defensive yield in the back half of the year.

headline: Paychex (PAYX) Q4 FY26 BEATS — Q4 rev +12.5% YoY to $1.61B (in-line), adj EPS $1.32 vs $1.31 cons (+0.9%); 45.5% EBITDA margin / 37.7% operating margin (vs 30.2% PY) confirms AI-driven operating leverage at SaaS-payroll; LAUNCHES 'WISE Workforce Intelligence Engine' AI platform
summary: Paychex reported fiscal Q4 2026 results Wednesday morning. Q4 revenue +12.5% YoY to $1.61B (in-line with consensus). Non-GAAP EPS $1.32 vs $1.31 cons (+0.9% beat). Adjusted EBITDA $729.7M with 45.5% margin. Operating margin 37.7%, sharply higher than 30.2% in the year-ago quarter — confirming meaningful AI-driven operating leverage on the underlying SaaS-payroll business. Capital return: $2.2B returned to shareholders in FY2026. Major strategic announcement: PAYX launched the 'WISE Workforce Intelligence Engine' AI platform, positioning the company as an AI-leadership story in the HR/payroll vertical. Read-through to House coverage: (1) PAYX is not in the AI-Datacenter-Power coverage universe but is a clean read on SaaS-vertical AI-driven operating leverage — the 750bp YoY operating-margin expansion is BEST-IN-CLASS for a non-mega-cap software name; (2) sympathetically positive for NOW, ADBE, INTU, CRM as the AI-cost-leverage thesis gains a clean print example; (3) confirms the Tuesday IBM Overweight upgrade thesis (JPM) that 'software AI-infra winners' are translating AI to GAAP earnings power; (4) modest tailwind for AMD / NVDA / AVGO on enterprise-SaaS AI-inference demand validation.
